chunk_summary: Defines a minimum screening dataset for undernutrition in under-five children, emphasizing age, weight, oedema, MUAC, and height/length for accurate classification and action planning during health contacts.
learning_objectives: Understand the components of the minimum screening dataset for undernutrition in under-five children.
Learn the importance of MUAC, oedema checks, and z-scores in identifying undernutrition.
Recognize the need for immediate classification and follow-up actions based on screening results.
Identify challenges in interpreting dehydration signs in severely wasted or oedematous children.
key_facts: Screening must include age, weight, oedema check (both feet, pitting), and MUAC for children 6–59 months.
MUAC <125 mm or any z-score < -2 SD indicates undernutrition and requires immediate action.
Length/height is essential for interpreting WHZ/WLZ and HAZ; weight-for-age alone can miss severe wasting.
During OPD sick visits, the triage set includes weight, MUAC, and oedema check, with height/length added when feasible.
Structured clinical assessment is critical in severe wasting or oedema to avoid misinterpreting dehydration signs.
reference_quote: ‘Measure MUAC in children 6–59 months at every contact and repeat the reading twice when the first value is abnormal or technique was suboptimal.’ (IMNCI Participants Module for Medical Officers, 2023)
document_key_topics: MUAC and oedema checks in under-five children
Action planning based on screening results
Challenges in interpreting dehydration in severe wasting
